title: Glossary
subtitle: Every term and symbol used across the BEARDOWN experiment — grouped, searchable, with equations in KaTeX.

terms.def: A 2-D image of frequency content over time. The visual modality fed to the CNN.
A model that consumes more than one input type at once — here a spectrogram image and a tabular feature vector.
A sub-network mapping one modality into a latent vector. BEARDOWN has two: a CNN and an MLP.
The compact learned vector an encoder produces — the modality reduced to a few hundred numbers.
Convolutional neural network. The spectrogram encoder (conv $\to$ ReLU $\to$ pool $\to$ flatten $\to$ dense).
Multilayer perceptron — stacked dense layers. The tabular encoder over the 58 audio features.
Combines the two embeddings into one vector — by concatenation or a learned gate. Deterministic.
Raw, unnormalized class scores produced just before the softmax.
Maps logits to a probability distribution over the 10 genres.
Belief about the head's weights before seeing data — a Gaussian whose width is set by $\texttt{prior\_scale}$.
The fitted (approximate) belief about the weights after training. Here a mean-field Gaussian $\mathcal{N}(\mu,\sigma^2)$.
The posterior factorizes per-weight (a diagonal Gaussian) — no modeled correlations between weights. Cheap, but geometrically thin.
How far $q$ sits from $p$; the regularizer that keeps the posterior honest.
Approximating an intractable true posterior with a simpler family $q_\phi$, fit by maximizing the ELBO.
Writing a sample so gradients flow through the randomness.
A variance-reduction scheme that decorrelates weight perturbations across a minibatch — cheaper, lower-noise gradients for the variational head.
Leaving dropout active at inference and averaging repeated passes — a cheap approximate posterior. BEARDOWN's alternative head variant.
Averaging over $T$ sampled passes to form the predictive distribution.
Model uncertainty from limited data — reducible by collecting more. The signal behind “more data, or am I done?”
Irreducible noise in the data itself (ambiguous clips, mislabels) — more data won't remove it.
Negative log-likelihood — the data-fit term of the loss. Low when the model assigns high probability to the true genre.
Evidence lower bound — the training objective. Maximizing it is identical to minimizing the (negative-ELBO) loss.
The gradient-descent algorithm that minimizes the loss — Adam / AdamW / RMSprop, chosen per sweep. Distinct from the ELBO, which is the objective.
Sweep knob that rescales the weight of the KL term in the loss, trading data-fit against regularization.
Rescaling each feature to mean 0, std 1. Fit on the train split only.
Clipping extreme outliers to percentile caps so a few wild values don't dominate the scaler.
Keeping every segment/representation of one recording in a single split, so the model can't memorize a song across train and test (the GTZAN leakage guard).
Splitting within each genre so all 10 classes keep their proportions across train / val / test.
When information about a test example reaches training — on GTZAN, segments of the same song landing in both splits, inflating accuracy (Sturm 2013).
Rotating train/validation over $k$ partitions and averaging — here $k=3$. Estimates stability, not just one-shot accuracy.
A randomized search over configurations (BEARDOWN: $n=20$) to find good settings without grid-searching everything.
Robust Ranking Metric — folds accuracy, CV stability, and posterior dispersion into one reliability-aware score. Q2's central contribution.
How spread-out the posterior is — wider means the model is less certain about its weights.
The set of configs where you can't gain accuracy without losing reliability (or vice-versa) — the honest tradeoff frontier.
Combining several metric rankings into a single consensus ordering of configurations.
